item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
The first image was taken by the author.

The second one is a photo by a Thai Bakery. As it has not been released under Creative Commons 3.0, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to see a GoI format on this pharma company

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fear of every pill-consuming person on the planet, skippified. Mad props on the medical trial section.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Who makes a cake like that?

I bet it tastes bloody good *ba bum tiss*

Any-who I thought it was a good article that suitably unnerved me. I thought that the testing logs added some good realism to it but how did it form two bodies, did the new one just appear, did it shed the old one or did it kind of grow from the first body?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is that photo really a cake? Because if so, hot damn.

I really like this. The effect's pretty neat, and definitely discomforting. I'm pleased with the thorough testing methods in the experiment log, too; it adds a nice touch of realism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… So…

…. So wait.

This is being a Time Lord in a pill.

… brilliant

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was half-expecting "The Stuff," from the eponymous film, and was almost not disappointed. As it is…it's…ok. I'm not impressed with the idea, but it's not bad enough to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy shit.

I was just reading that comic, it reminded me to check on this comment XD

Coincidence?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I find myself really enjoying this SPC; it's well-written and the concept is delightfully morbid. Also kudos on the cake person. It looks horrible in an awesome way.

You got my upvote, regardless

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're on to something shady and hidden with thie SPC

I'm stupid

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: http://www.smbc-comics.com/?id=3324#comic

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just googled the image, and it IS cake

… Wonder how it tasted :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
one small problem with this SCP

"Analyses found no legitimate chemical components"

Is this saying that there were no chemicals inside the pill?
Does this pill contain another form of matter?

Because that would contradict this statement.

"analysis of the pills' contents show that each capsule is apparently filled with objects ground into powder and filled into capsules, including glass, quartz, plant matter, volcanic ash, sugar, bone meal, and owl pellets. "

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting effect, written nicely. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvote removed. Though I'm still not fond of "Sunrise Care Practitioners".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I removed interview

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it is essentially a counterfeit medical substance problem, and anyone working for pharma will tell you that there are places on this planet where it's next to impossible to manage this problem.

Foundation's "normality enforcement" is global, and there are places where containing this is almost impossible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting concept described well. End of the interview is maybe a little lolFoundation, but my big question is, why Keter?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This… I… no. No. THIS CANNOT BE CAKE! CAKE! Nooooo…

*sobs helplessly

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All humans are cake

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted due to good medical science. Congrats. Really.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
NOTHING EVER IMPRESSES YOU, DAD, GAWWWWWD!~!!!

I'm strongly considering removing the interview, actually.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fun fact: the one in the picture isn't a dead body. It's a cake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Firstly, how the hell does this only have one comment? Read the articles, people.

This edged juuuust into downvote territory for me. The idea of varying degrees of depersonalization through taking some weird pill, combined with the body horror (nice pic, by the way) is a solid one, and I liked this article in general concept.

There are some execution issues, though. I don't care for the containment; I'm not sure how much value the Foundation would get out of a general anti-knockoff drug awareness campaign. That would require a pretty big outlay of resources, since this is an international phenomenon, without more than an incidental benefit in containment. Also, the "SCP" initialed front company. I used to sort of brush this aside as a personal gripe, but the more I think about it the more I'm convinced that this is an incredibly stupid practice for an organization that is generally accepted to care at least somewhat about secrecy. This is going to start factoring into my voting.

I don't like the SCP-500 reference. There's not more than a tenuous connection (which is sort of even acknowledged in the denial) and therefore no real reason for it.

Finally, the interview strikes me as clutter. We're not really finding out anything new about the skip, and the flippant tone undercuts the only other potential source of impact it could have (a personal narrative adding an immediate emotional dimension to the otherwise clinical document).

The good news is that this strikes me as extremely improvable. With some tweaks and revisions this could be really good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A place for my stuffheavily inspired by the movie "The Gate" and would have been a lot worse and more of a ripoff if not for Dr Yucatan
